text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy village nestled between hills, there lived an owl named Olive. Olive loved nothing more than watching the sun dip BELOW the oak trees.

Every evening she'd glide silently through the whispering leaves, hunting for insects. One special time each week, she'd swoop down to the river nearby. There she liked to fish. Not fancy fishing rods—I just liked fishing with my toes.

One particular evening the river was unusually sluggish. That's when Olive saw it—not a fish.

A round, soft thing bobbing there. It couldn't be...

That was no fish.

Olive hoot-ed softly. It was a soft pink tail! A little pink owl?

She drew nearer. Oh wow!

It was just a baby.

She thought.

She'd keep it.

She tucked the precious thing into an old sock.

Every night.

Every night Olive watched starlight through the sock. The owl knew her.

When spring came those delicate feathers grew bold.

Olive
